Title: Stacking AsFMT overexpression with BdPMT loss of function enhances monolignol ferulate production in Brachypodium distachyon

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Zea mays ubiquitin-1 promoter with intron, constitutive
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monolignol ferulate conjugate formation during lignification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point/side-route acylation step in lignin monomer modification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms monolignol ferulate conjugates incorporated into lignin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Monolignol ferulates (ML-FA; including sinapyl ferulate and guaiacyl ferulate conjugates)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers feruloyl group from feruloyl-CoA to monolignols to form monolignol ferulate esters that are incorporated into lignin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point/enzyme abundance limitation for ML-FA production in Brachypodium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in various organs in 10-day-old seedlings and 31-day-old plants from constitutive transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Introduces ester-linked lignin units that may improve biomass processing/digestibility for biorefinery and forage applications
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased lignin-bound ML-FA about 16-fold over wild type; when stacked with Bdpmt-1, increased ML-FA about 32-fold over wild type
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Heterologous AsFMT was functional in Brachypodium and substantially increased lignin-bound ML-FA. 2. AsFMT alone caused about a 16-fold increase in ML-FA without obvious growth defects or major lignin-content changes. 3. Stacking AsFMT with Bdpmt-1 further boosted ML-FA to about 32-fold over wild type, showing competition with PMT pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression/transformation; EYFP fusion fluorescence; Western blot; DFRC lignin compositional analysis; genetic stacking by crossing; metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological defects; no significant improvement in saccharification on its own; stacked with Bdccr1-1 partially recovered recalcitrance via lignin restoration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target to increase lignin-incorporated ML-FA in grasses; best deployed together with knockout of competing PMT pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AsFMT was chosen instead of native BdFMT/OsFMT to avoid possible sense suppression; EYFP C-terminal fusion gave strongest expression and was used for analysis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monolignol p-coumarate conjugate formation during lignification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Competing branch acylation step for monolignol and p-coumaroyl-CoA utilization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with ML-FA branch for shared monolignol substrate and upstream hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA flux
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers p-coumaroyl group from p-coumaroyl-CoA to monolignols to form monolignol p-coumarate esters incorporated into lignin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as a competing branch enzyme rather than direct positive controller of ML-FA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Higher ML-FA incorporation is desired for improved biomass processing potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function alone did not increase ML-FA, but in combination with AsFMT overexpression enabled a 32-fold ML-FA increase and reduced ML-pCA to trace levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bdpmt-1 knockout eliminated most ML-pCA production. 2. Bdpmt-1 alone did not alter native ML-FA levels. 3. Removing BdPMT strongly enhanced AsFMT-driven ML-FA accumulation, indicating substrate competition between PMT and FMT pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis; crossing with AsFMT overexpressor; DFRC lignin compositional analysis; CAPS genotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Bdpmt-1 background showed increased tricin and reduced ABSL in prior characterization; no improved glucose release in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent knockout target to combine with FMT overexpression for maximizing ML-FA incorporation into grass lignin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bdpmt-1 is a sodium azide-induced point mutant with G to A substitution at position 563 of the ORF

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream/core flux-generating step toward monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Controls precursor flux toward monolignols and thereby indirectly affects monolignol ferulate formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters to corresponding cinnamaldehydes in monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important core flux step for lignin biosynthesis, but not established here as effective control point for increasing ML-FA in Brachypodium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Bdccr1-1 mutant showed reduced transcript abundance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed in culm plus leaf sheath tissue of apical internode
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Engineering lignin-related traits for digestibility/biorefinery performance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Partial loss of BdCCR1 reduced lignin content and increased saccharification but did not increase ML-FA, either alone or beyond AsFMT effect when stacked
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bdccr1-1 is a partial-loss-of-function allele with reduced CCR1 expression and reduced lignin content. 2. Bdccr1-1 alone did not change ML-FA or ML-pCA levels relative to wild type. 3. Stacking Bdccr1-1 with AsFMT did not further elevate ML-FA above AsFMT alone, indicating CCR1 reduction was insufficient to reroute flux to ML-FA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant characterization; RT-semiquantitative PCR; Klason lignin assay; thioacidolysis; phloroglucinol staining; DFRC; saccharification assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard growth chamber conditions; partial saccharification after 1 mM or 6 mM NaOH pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced lignin content increased glucose release, but stacking with AsFMT partially restored lignin and reduced digestibility benefit
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reducing lignin and improving saccharification, but not effective in this study for boosting ML-FA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bdccr1-1 T-DNA insertion is in third intron; recessive partial-loss-of-function allele
